title: Weighted Gini
description: Learn how weighted Gini combines impurity across left and right child nodes.
takeaways: A split creates left and right child groups.
Weighted Gini averages child impurities by group size.
The best split minimizes weighted Gini impurity.

practiceLab.objective: Compute child-node Gini values, form weighted Gini for Age thresholds, compare candidates, and select the best split.
instructions: Run the dataset cell and inspect the eight-patient Age / heart_disease table.
Run the helpers cell to define gini_impurity and weighted_gini.
Run the guided split at Age <= 51.5 and record parent, child, and weighted Gini.
Complete Exercise 1 for threshold 46.5 and store wg_46.
Print the comparison table of weighted Gini scores.
Identify and print the best split (lowest weighted Gini).
Answer the reflection questions about weighting by sample counts.
hints: Left group uses age <= threshold; right uses age > threshold.
The best split minimizes weighted Gini.
practiceLab.expectedOutput: Printed parent/child Gini for 51.5, a weighted Gini for 46.5, a three-row candidate table, and a best-split dictionary with the lowest weighted_gini.
